title: trifolium argutum var. cruentum
intro: Trifolium argutum var. cruentum, also known as bloody clover, is a variety of the Trifolium argutum plant, a perennial herb in the legume family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Bloody clover is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. Bloody clover can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used as a component in topical ointments and creams for skin conditions.
